title: An `if ( condition )` construct shall be followed by a compound statement. The else keyword shall be followed by either a compound statement, or another if statement
description: An `if ( condition )` construct shall be followed by a compound statement. The else keyword shall be followed by either a compound statement, or another if statementAnalyze your codeintentionality-clearmaintainabilityCode Smellmisra-c++2008Why is this an issue?More InfoThis rule is a strict implementation of a MISRA (Motor Industry Software Reliability Association) rule. MISRA defines best practices for developing safety-critical software.
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.

noncompliant:
if (condition)
    doSomething(); // Noncompliant: not a compound statement

checks.message: Use compound statements (braces) for if/else constructs to improve readability and prevent errors
